policy_refs: [CS-RULE-001, CS-RULE-002, CS-RULE-003, CS-RULE-004, CS-RULE-005, SEC-RULE-001, SEC-RULE-002, SEC-RULE-007, CS-RULE-018, CS-RULE-019, CS-RULE-020, CS-RULE-021, CS-RULE-022]
kb_refs: [Section 4.3 - Multi-Factor Authentication, Section 4.6 - Authenticator App Reset]
expected_outcome: Customer lost the phone with her authenticator app and cannot complete two-factor sign-in. Aisha completes full 3-of-5 verification, removes the old 2FA enrollment, walks the customer through enrolling a fresh authenticator on her new phone, and confirms login. PASS — SOP-04 + SEC-RULE-007 satisfied on-call (no store visit required because the customer cleared phone-based verification). No ticket required. Case ref + survey provided.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling NexaLink Telecommunications Broadband and Support. My name is Aisha. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And this call may be recorded for quality assurance and training purposes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Hi Aisha, I'm Emma Caraway. I'm in a bit of a panic — my phone was stolen yesterday, I got a new one this morning, and now I can't get into my account because the two-factor codes are still going to the old phone.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Oh, Emma, I'm so sorry that happened. Losing your phone is stressful enough without being locked out of accounts on top of it. Take a breath — this is absolutely something I can sort out with you on this call.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I understand you're calling about a two-factor authentication recovery, and I can certainly help you with that. Before I touch anything on the account, I'll need to verify your identity. Could you start with the account number?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes. One-nine-zero-eight, seven-seven-six-two, four-three.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you. And could you confirm the last four digits of the payment method on file?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Last four are three-three-eight-four.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Perfect. And to complete a third identifier — could you confirm the full name on the account, exactly as it appears?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Emma Lillian Caraway.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That matches. You're fully verified, Emma.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Oh, thank you.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: You're welcome. Now I want to walk you through what we're going to do, before we do it, so nothing comes as a surprise.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Your data is used solely for service provisioning and support quality, in accordance with our Privacy Policy. Because you've cleared identity verification by phone, I can complete the two-factor reset on this call — you won't need to visit a store. I'll remove the old authenticator enrollment from your account, and then together we'll set up a fresh enrollment on your new phone.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: That would be amazing. Thank you.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Of course. Before I unenroll the old one — do you have the new phone in your hand right now? And do you have an authenticator app already, or would you like a recommendation?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: I have the new phone, yes. I don't have an authenticator app yet — what do you recommend?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Either Google Authenticator or Microsoft Authenticator will work, and they're both free. Whichever you prefer. They behave identically for our setup, so just go with what feels natural.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: I'll grab Google Authenticator. Installing it now.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Take your time. There's no rush at all.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay, it's installed and open. It's asking me to scan a code or enter a setup key.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Perfect — that's exactly the screen we want. I'm going to unenroll the old phone now and generate a fresh setup key for your account. One moment.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Removing the old enrollment.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Done. The old phone can no longer generate valid codes for your account, which is what you want given that it's no longer in your possession.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Oh, that's such a relief.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I thought you'd feel that way. Now generating a fresh enrollment for the new phone.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Okay. I have a setup key ready. I'm going to read it to you in groups of four characters — type each group into the authenticator app's setup key field exactly as I say it, lowercase letters and digits as I read them.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay, ready.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Group one: x-seven-q-m. Group two: r-two-n-p. Group three: t-eight-f-j. Group four: w-five-k-d.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I'm still here with you, Emma — just take your time typing those in. Let me know when you've got all four groups.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay, all four are in. It's showing me a six-digit code now.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Perfect — that means the enrollment is connected. Read me the six digits showing right now so I can verify the pairing on my side.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Four-one-eight, seven-two-six.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And matched. The new phone is officially your two-factor device.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Oh, thank you so much.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Would you like to try logging into your account right now while we're on the line, just so we both know it works?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yes, please.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Okay — username, password, it asked for the code, I put in the current code, and… I'm in. It worked.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's exactly what we wanted to see. You're fully set up.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And just to give you the broader picture — the issue here wasn't anything wrong with your account. The old phone simply had the only valid two-factor secret, and once it was gone, only a manual reset like the one we just did could restore access. That's by design — it's what keeps your account safe.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: That makes sense. I'm relieved it wasn't more complicated.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: To summarize: you reported your authenticator was on a phone that was stolen, I verified your identity using three of five identifiers, removed the old two-factor enrollment, walked you through enrolling a fresh authenticator on the new phone, and confirmed you can log in. No follow-up is required on your end. Does that all sound right?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Is there anything else I can help you with today, Emma?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: No, that's all. Honestly, thank you for being so calm about it — you really helped.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: That's what I'm here for. Your case reference number is N-X-L dash one-five-five-zero-nine-one-two. You'll receive a brief survey after we hang up — if you have a moment, we'd really appreciate the feedback. Take care, Emma.
